blockingIssues.description: CompositorConfig struct is 64 bytes (not 60) due to repr(C) alignment padding from u64 fields. IPC PAYLOAD_SIZE is 60 bytes. from_payload() copies 64 bytes into a 60-byte payload (4-byte buffer overwrite). payload_as() reads 64 bytes from a 60-byte payload (4-byte out-of-bounds read). Both are undefined behavior. The struct exists in both compositor/main.rs and init/main.rs — both copies must be fixed. The debug_assert that checks size <= PAYLOAD_SIZE is compiled away in release builds. Fix options: (1) reorder fields to pack u32s together and add explicit _pad field to control layout, (2) manually serialize/deserialize instead of using from_payload/payload_as, (3) split the config into two messages.

nonBlockingIssues.description: MAX_SURFACES=16 silently truncates excess surfaces. Currently using 6, so not a problem.
Damage tracking effectively disabled (full-screen transfer every frame). Performance regression from M1's partial transfers, but reasonable given multi-surface compositing touches most pixels.
CompositeSurface construction duplicated between initial frame render and event loop (~2 locations).
First-frame content surface mostly transparent (incremental clearing). Visually imperceptible since bg colors differ by only ~6 units per channel.
TEXT_INSET_BOTTOM naming could better document its relationship to STATUS_BAR_H.
Potential u32 overflow in fill_gradient_v for heights >16.8M pixels. Not reachable in practice.
proportional_string_width() duplicates width-measurement logic from draw_proportional_string() in the drawing library.
IHDR interlace method not checked — Adam7-interlaced PNG would silently decode incorrectly instead of returning error.
Silent 64-IDAT-chunk limit. Excess chunks ignored, causing downstream DataSizeMismatch error.
png_decode_test_image_from_file test silently passes when test.png is missing (uses if let Ok).
Transient double heap allocation during PNG decode (~65KB decode_buf + ~65KB image_pixels). With bump allocator, decode_buf memory never reclaimed.
Test uses hardcoded 128x128 buffer size assumption for test.png.
Double sys::wait syscall per loop iteration when timer is active (blocking wait + extra poll). Cheap but unnecessary.
Silent failure if timer recreation fails after old timer closed. No serial log diagnostic.
Redundant content surface re-render when switching from image back to editor (rendered twice per switch).
Duplicated keycode-to-ASCII map in test file (copy of input driver's map). Maintenance risk if canonical map changes.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md: compositor description now mentions timer channel, multi-surface compositing, dual content modes (editor/image), and F1 context switching
Updated architecture.md: Drawing Library section now lists fill_gradient_v, CompositeSurface/composite_surfaces, and png_decode. Removed stale 'no gradients yet' claim.
Updated architecture.md: Font Pipeline section now notes shared buffer also contains PNG image data (not just fonts)
Added Multi-Surface Compositing section to architecture.md documenting the 6-surface z-ordered model, dedicated render functions pattern, and content mode switching

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add coding convention: 'repr(C) struct alignment: When using IPC from_payload/payload_as with repr(C) structs, verify size_of::<T>() (not just sum of field sizes) is <= 60. Struct size rounds up to the alignment of the largest field (u64 → 8-byte alignment). Use a const assertion or manual byte layout to guarantee fit.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: proportional-font feature added prop_font_len (u32) to CompositorConfig, making fields sum to 60 bytes but actual struct size 64 bytes due to u64 alignment padding. Worker believed it was 60 bytes. This caused a 4-byte IPC buffer overflow — real UB.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update test baseline count from '719+ tests' to '789+ tests' (768 system + 21 prototype as of beautiful-chrome-and-content completion).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: AGENTS.md currently says '719+ tests'. Actual count after M2: 789.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: os-worker skill should mention the 16 KiB stack constraint more prominently in its constraints section, including the -C opt-level=s compiler flag. Workers keep hitting this indirectly.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: PNG decoder worker hit stack overflow with initial Huffman table approach (~98KB). Had to completely redesign. Same issue affected M1's gamma-correct-blending worker. Architecture.md documents it but the skill's constraints section doesn't quantify it.

rejectedObservations.observation: architecture.md should note timer as third event source in compositor
rejectedObservations.reason: Already addressed in appliedUpdates — compositor description now mentions timer channel

rejectedObservations.observation: IPC from_payload uses only debug_assert for size checking (compiled away in release)
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a real concern but the fix is a code change (add const assertion), not a documentation/convention change. The blocking issue for proportional-font will force this to be addressed in the fix.

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicated keycode map between input driver and test should use shared constants
rejectedObservations.reason: Known architectural limitation — no_std bare-metal binaries can't easily share code with host test crates. The duplication pattern is established and accepted. Adding a cross-reference comment would help but isn't a convention change.
